question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a single request whose resulting error carries an extremely large `err.message` string (e.g., via a URL parameter that gets embedded in an InvalidArgumentError), causing the template-literal log line `${req.ip} ${req.method} ${req.originalUrl} in ${elapsed} ms: ${statusCode} ${err.constructor.name} ${errorMessage}` to allocate and serialize megabytes of string data per request, increasing CPU and memory consumption by over 30% compared to baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: shouldPrintStacktrace()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an error whose `err.message` is a very large string (hundreds of kilobytes) so that the regex `simpleErrors.test(err.message)` (pattern `/statement timeout/`) must scan the entire string on every error, causing repeated regex evaluation overhead that measurably increases CPU consumption beyond 30% of baseline?
